cataloging_subject.label: Companion to the English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from Carl Horstmann's Edition
cataloging_subject.scope_note: A University of Michigan/CME electronic companion selection, encoded in 2003 from Carl Horstmann's 1895-1896 two-volume Yorkshire Writers edition. It is not a single medieval work and is not a collected-works container whose forty-nine intellectual items may be blanket-attributed to Richard Rolle. The cataloging boundary is 7 editorial groups, 44 DIV2 containers, and exactly 49 intellectual items; the four split containers remain explicit.

catalog_summary.title: Companion to the English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from Carl Horstmann's Edition
catalog_summary.author: Multiple anonymous and differently attributed authors (not blanket Richard Rolle)
catalog_summary.editor: Carl Horstmann; University of Michigan electronic editors
catalog_summary.date: medieval items, chiefly late fourteenth-fifteenth century; source edition 1895-1896; electronic selection 2003
catalog_summary.region: England; northern, West Midlands, and southern dialect/witness associations; London early-print carriers

identifiers.scheme: University of Michigan Digital Collections identifier

language_statements.label: Latin

scope.description: Genuine embedded quotations, prayers, or source-language passages; editorial-only Latin headings and imprints are excluded.

notes: Part scope was derived from FOREIGN content inside prose/verse text containers, with split DIV2 passages assigned to the correct intellectual item.

form_statements.description: Discovery form for the 49-item companion remains prose. Four Middle English verse lines in p24 and four in p33 make those two items mixed-form without changing the whole-selection discovery facet.
The Mirror of Saint Edmund is prose with a four-line verse argument opening its Vernon witness stream.
Forms of Confession is prose with four embedded verse opening lines.

summaries.value: A human-readable companion selection of forty-nine primarily prose Middle English works, excerpts, translations, and witness versions historically gathered around Richard Rolle. The record keeps anonymous, disputed, source-layer, and Walter Hilton responsibilities separate and links each item through the 2003 U-M encoding and Horstmann's 1895-1896 edition to its manuscript or early-print stream.

Seven source/editorial groups contain forty-four XML DIV2 carriers but forty-nine count-once items because Narrationes Legendariae, the two Abbey works, the 1508 Remedy volume, and Profits of Tribulation each contain multiple intellectual works. Six Masters and Twelve Profits remain distinct.

extent.description: Seven editorial groups and forty-four encoded DIV2 containers map to forty-nine count-once intellectual items. Forty-seven are prose-only; p24 and p33 are mixed, each with four embedded verse lines. The whole-selection discovery form remains prose, with both exceptions retained at part and extent level.

content_parts.label: Prose Treatises of MS Rawlinson C 285
content_parts.summary: Fifteenth-century English parchment codex; the selected prose is chiefly northern in language.
notes: Editorial source group containing 12 encoded DIV2 containers and 12 count-once intellectual items.

content_parts.summary: anonymous Middle English author. Genres: devotional instruction, charity.
notes: Layer-qualified date note: medieval; copied in a fifteenth-century witness. Copying and print dates describe carriers unless the wording explicitly dates the work, source, or translation.
Dialect, witness, or print association: northern English witness/language. This is not promoted to a blanket place of composition or cultural origin.
Primary source stream(s): ms:bodleian:Rawl-C-285

content_parts.label: Four Profitable Things

First of two independent intellectual works in DIV2 7.5. Horstmann's p. 389 is editorial introduction; the work text on p. 390 is printed from Royal 17 A XXV f. 62, with Harley 1706 ff. 54v-66v and other copies collateral. Allen notes a probable connection with a similar German piece circulating with Albert of Cologne.

content_parts.label: The Twelve Profits of Tribulation

notes: The title records an editorial history and must not be normalized into blanket Richard Rolle authorship.
The XML contains a Twelve Profits item in group 7; the header's accidental-omission note concerns another Royal 17 B XVII Twelve Profits stream and does not erase p40.
Witness copying dates and dialect regions remain qualified notes or lineage facts unless scholarship supports a work-level composition or translation assertion.
All access, local-cache, facsimile-target, and rights statements are maintained in the linked lineage manifest.
